name: Lemon Butter Mousse
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small heatproof bowl, combine the first amount of lemon juice and the grated lemon zest. Sprinkle the gelatin over this mixture and set aside to soften for 10 minutes.
2. Place the bowl in a saucepan with hot water, ensuring it doesn't touch the heat. Stir until the gelatin is completely dissolved. Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature.
3. In a large bowl, whip cream until just stiff. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.
4. In a separate deep bowl, beat egg yolks with salt until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1 cup of confectioners' sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
5. Beat butter until soft and fluffy. Add 1 cup of confectioners' sugar and beat until smooth. Alternately fold in the dissolved gelatin and remaining confectioners' sugar.
6. Gently combine the beaten egg yolks with half of the whipped cream. Fold in the remaining whipped cream.
7. Stir in the additional lemon juice into the whipped cream and gently incorporate it into the mousse. Transfer to a serving dish or stemmed wine glasses, cover, and refrigerate until chilled and set, approximately 3 hours.
Serves 10 to 12
